summary: Tested GPS Mileage Tracker and Financials Mileage consolidation. Found 1 critical bug: /api/financials/summary/{year} endpoint still reads from legacy mileage_entries collection instead of gps_trips. 9 frontend E2E tests passed, 8/9 backend tests passed.

issues.description: Financial summary endpoint /api/financials/summary/{year} still reads mileage from legacy mileage_entries collection instead of gps_trips collection. This causes inconsistent mileage data between GPS Mileage Tracker (shows 12.51 miles) and Financials summary (shows 5.0 miles).
issues.reproduction:
1. Call GET /api/financials/mileage/2026 - returns 12.51 miles
2. Call GET /api/financials/summary/2026 - returns 5.0 miles in deductions.mileage_miles
3. The values should match but don't because get_financial_summary() reads from db.mileage_entries instead of db.gps_trips

action_items: Fix /app/backend/app/routers/financials.py line 692-695: Change get_financial_summary() to read mileage from gps_trips collection instead of mileage_entries, similar to how get_mileage() and get_tax_prep_summary() already do it
seed_data_creation: No new seed data created. Tests use existing production data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: GPS Mileage Tracker UI is working correctly - YTD summary shows trips, miles, deduction with correct IRS rate ($0.725/mile for 2026). Manual trip entry form works. The bug is in the backend: get_financial_summary() at line 692 in financials.py reads from mileage_entries instead of gps_trips. The fix should mirror how get_mileage() (line 449-508) and get_tax_prep_summary() (line 227-252) already correctly read from gps_trips collection.

gps_mileage_tracker_ytd_summary.details: YTD summary displays trips (4), miles (12.5), deduction ($9) correctly

manual_trip_entry_form.details: Form opens, has date/miles/purpose fields, save button enables when filled

tax_deduction_calculation.details: IRS rate $0.725/mile for 2026 is correct, deduction calculated properly

financials_mileage_endpoint.details: GET /api/financials/mileage/2026 returns correct data from gps_trips

financials_summary_mileage.details: GET /api/financials/summary/2026 returns wrong mileage (5.0 instead of 12.51) - reads from legacy mileage_entries instead of gps_trips
